pattern: Each difficulty is independent: with batches of two or three, the fewest batches for a count is ceil(count/3), and a lone task is impossible.
hints: Tasks of different difficulties never share a round, so solve each difficulty's count on its own and add up the rounds.
For a single difficulty with c tasks you want the fewest groups of size 2 or 3 that sum to c; a count of exactly 1 can never be grouped.
Any count of at least 2 is achievable, and the minimum number of groups is the ceiling of c divided by 3; return -1 if any count is 1.

edge_inputs.description: a difficulty with a single task is impossible
count of four needs two rounds, not one
count of five splits into three plus two

stress.description: many tasks drawn from a small difficulty pool so most counts are large and groupable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    pool = rng.randint(1, 50)
    tasks = [rng.randint(1, pool) * 7 for _ in range(n)]
    return [tasks]
